column1: # MathSolver printer torture corpus -- plain & LaTeX rendering (DESIGN.md section 5).
# Same TSV format as cases.tsv; see tests/acceptance/README.md for columns and
# matching semantics (id, subcommand, input, extra_args, expected_kind, expected, expects_exit).
# The printer is exercised through the CLI subcommands that echo a rendering:
# simplify). Every input here is a fixpoint of simplify (or its simplified
# form is exactly the shape under test), so the expectations hold whether or
# not the `latex` subcommand simplifies before printing.
# Spec-freedom assumptions (derived ONLY from DESIGN.md, freedom noted):
# 1. Plain spacing/operator style is pinned by the section 5 worked examples
#    spaces around binary +/- in an Add, tight "*" and "^". `exact` is used
#    only for shapes those examples cover.
# 2. LaTeX juxtaposition may or may not carry a space ("2x" vs "2 x"), and
#    single-digit/single-letter exponents may or may not be braced ("x^{2}"
#    vs "x^2"); regexes accept both -- EXCEPT where DESIGN prints a literal
# 3. Add display tie-break: section 5 says ties break by "reverse
#    compare_expr", but its own worked example "x + (-2)*y -> x - 2*y"
#    implies the opposite order for this Symbol-vs-Mul degree tie. pm-02 pins
#    the literal worked example; the pure tie cases (po-06, po-07) accept
#    either order while still rejecting wrong operators/signs/merging. If
#    pm-02 fails on term order alone, reconcile with the section 5 example
#    before touching the printer (cf. README: widen, don't churn).
# 4. Mul factors are assumed printed in canonical AST arg order (section 2
#    sort: Number < Constant < Symbol < Pow < Function < Mul < Add) -- only
#    Add has a print-time reordering in section 5. Hence "(x+1)*y" prints
# 5. sy-03: the plain rendering of symbol x_max must keep braces, "x_{max}" --
#    the unbraced "x_max" would re-lex as x_m*a*x (section 4 subscript rule),
#    violating the section 5 round-trip invariant. x_12 stays unbraced
#    (section 4 pins that the Plain printer emits "x_12").
# 6. Plain division rendering splits a non-integer rational coefficient into
# 7. Pow(E, r) beats sqrt/division reconstruction for EVERY exponent
#    and inside a Mul the factor stays in the numerator: "x/e^2" -> "x*e^(-2)".
# All expectations were cross-checked numerically with python3 (two sample
# points per case; see scratchpad generator).
# ---------- sqrt reconstruction, plain (Pow(u,1/2) -> sqrt, others stay ^) ----------
# ---------- Add display: descending total degree, Numbers last ----------
# degree ties: order not pinned (see header note 3) -- either order, right operators
# x^(y^z): '^' is right-assoc, so parens are optional here (minimal = none)
# digit-boundary \cdot rule -- literal worked example, required verbatim
# juxtaposition must stay re-parseable: '\pi r' needs its space, '2x'/'x2^{x}' do not need \cdot
